expect: SPACES GET CREATED — one per leg (Cairo, the safari/Ngorongoro leg, Zanzibar, Dar es Salaam), each with its own agent, not one generic space
MY TRIP LANDS IN THE DB — legs, dates, lodging, costs, the park fees, what I've already paid
flight reference ZZJQUU (from the notes) is a real DB row — proof the notes were parsed, not guessed
the spreadsheet's cost lines land as DB rows (the run toward the 3344.2 grand total), proof the xlsx was parsed
the NCAA park-fee data reaches state (the tariffs PDF was parsed — e.g. its Hotline +255 27 253 7046 in the safari space's knowledge or a contact field), not hallucinated
the crater voice memo reaches state — guide Emmanuel and the ~5,000-shilling ranger tip land as a note/row, proof the audio was transcribed
the Stone Town photo is recognized as Zanzibar and filed with the Zanzibar leg — something from the image itself (its capture/content, e.g. Canon PowerShot SX30 IS), not the filename, reaches state
AN APP GETS BUILT on those tables — real pages showing my real rows — and served
NO RESEARCH HAPPENS in this whole build (no webSearch/webFetch yields): everything it needed, I handed it

turns.lastText: {"type":"Stack","props":{"gap":2},"children":[{"type":"Heading","props":{"level":2},"children":["All done — your trip is in one place now"]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Everything you sent me is now organized into your Tanzania trip app. You can open it at ",{"type":"Code","props":{},"children":["/app/tanzania-trip-organization/"]}," on your phone or desktop."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Here's what landed where:"]},{"type":"Table","props":{"columns":["What you had","Where it lives now"],"rows":[["tanzaniamemories.md — your journal","trip_notes table — all your notes, contacts, and recommendations, searchable"],["trip-costs.xlsx — your cost tracker","trip_costs table — every line item seeded from the spreadsheet, with running totals"],["ngorongoro tariff PDF","park_tariffs table — the official rates, right alongside your costs so you can check your budget"],["Stone Town photo","On the app home page — that warm courtyard with the carved wooden balconies"],["Voice memo — crater day","trip_days table — Emmanuel, the bracelets (~TZS 35,000), and the ranger-tip question flagged as outstanding"]]},"children":[]},{"type":"Callout","props":{"variant":"warning","title":"Don't forget"},"children":["The ranger tip question (~TZS 5,000 cash at the gate) is flagged in your trip days. Ask Richard about it ",{"type":"Strong","props":{},"children":["before"]}," you make the safari balance payment — nobody mentioned it upfront, and you'll want that settled."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["There are also four specialist spaces behind the scenes — one for each leg of your trip (Cairo stopover, Arusha, the northern safari circuit, and Zanzibar) — so if you ask me something later about any part of the trip, the answer comes from your own material, not a web search."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Open it up, take a look, and let me know if anything needs adjusting. You're sorted."]}]}

park_tariffs.description: Crater service fee — per vehicle descent
Ranger/guide fee (sometimes expected as cash tip at gate)
park_tariffs.source: Verbal — Emmanuel, crater day. UNCONFIRMED — verify with Richard.

trip_costs.notes: Booked through Richard. Balance payment pending.
Per the official tariff PDF. Crater service fee + conservation fee.
Extra ranger tip ~TZS 5,000 — Emmanuel mentioned this. Separate from park fees. UNCONFIRMED — ask Richard.

trip_costs.description: Two Maasai beaded bracelets for Athena Mari
trip_costs.notes: Purchased from a Maasai lady at the crater rim. ~$20-25 USD.
Stopover on the way to Tanzania

trip_days.title: Arrival in Arusha
trip_days.summary: Arrived in Tanzania. Met Richard at the safari office for briefing.

trip_days.title: Ngorongoro Crater Day
trip_days.summary: The crater day — driver Emmanuel. Rhino sightings, massive herds. Bought Maasai bracelets at the rim. Ranger tip question came up.
trip_days.highlights: Rhino sightings, Maasai bracelets for Athena Mari (TZS 35,000), Emmanuel the guide, ranger tip question (~TZS 5,000)

trip_days.title: Stone Town, Zanzibar
trip_days.summary: Flew to Zanzibar. Wandered Stone Town — white stucco buildings, ornate dark wooden balconies, courtyard guesthouse.
trip_days.highlights: Courtyard guesthouse, spice markets, waterfront sunset

trip_notes.title: Trip Planning & Preparation
trip_notes.body: Tanzania trip planning phase — vaccinations, visas, flights booked, safari operator selected (Richard). Packing list assembled.

trip_notes.title: Cairo Stopover
trip_notes.body: Stopover in Cairo en route to Tanzania. Explored the city briefly.

trip_notes.title: Arusha Pre-Safari
trip_notes.body: Arrived in Arusha. Met with Richard at the safari office. Briefed on the itinerary — Tarangire, Manyara, Ngorongoro, Serengeti.

trip_notes.title: Northern Tanzania Safari Circuit
trip_notes.body: The main safari: Tarangire National Park (elephants, baobabs), Lake Manyara (tree-climbing lions), Ngorongoro Crater (the highlight — rhino sightings, massive herds), Serengeti (endless plains, wildebeest).

trip_notes.title: Zanzibar
trip_notes.body: Flew to Zanzibar after the safari. Stone Town — winding alleys, spice markets, the waterfront at sunset. Stayed in a courtyard guesthouse with white stucco walls and ornate dark wooden balconies.

trip_notes.title: Richard — Safari Operator
trip_notes.body: Main contact for the safari. Based in Arusha. Handled all bookings: parks, accommodations, driver/guide.

trip_notes.title: Emmanuel — Driver/Guide
trip_notes.body: Driver and guide for the Ngorongoro crater day. Very knowledgeable. Not Richard — a separate guide assigned for the crater.

trip_notes.title: Ask Richard about ranger tip
trip_notes.body: Emmanuel mentioned an extra ranger tip sometimes expected at the Ngorongoro gate: ~TZS 5,000 cash, separate from park fees. Nobody told us about this beforehand. Need to ask Richard before settling the safari balance payment.

trip_notes.title: Maasai Bracelets for Athena Mari
trip_notes.body: At the Ngorongoro crater rim, a Maasai lady sold two beaded bracelets for Athena Mari. Paid roughly TZS 35,000 (~$20–25 USD). Beautiful beaded work.

trip_notes.title: Emmanuel as a guide
trip_notes.body: Emmanuel was an excellent guide — very knowledgeable about the crater, wildlife, and Maasai culture. Would recommend requesting him specifically.

schema.title: Park Tariffs
schema.description: Official Ngorongoro Conservation Area park-fee tariff reference for budgeting.

fee_type.description: conservation_fee | crater_service_fee | camping_fee | vehicle_fee | ranger_fee | other

schema.title: Trip Costs
schema.description: All trip expenses from the cost-tracking spreadsheet.

category.description: flights | accommodation | safari | park_fees | food | transport | souvenirs | other

schema.title: Trip Days
schema.description: Day-by-day itinerary tying together notes, costs, photos, and locations.

day_number.description: Day number in the trip sequence

schema.title: Trip Notes
schema.description: Journal entries, contacts, recommendations, and trip narrative.

body.description: Full text of the note or entry

contact_name.description: Name if this is a contact entry

contact_phone.description: Phone number if this is a contact entry

contact_email.description: Email if this is a contact entry

location.description: Where this note relates to (Arusha, Ngorongoro, Zanzibar, etc.)

priority.description: low | medium | high — for loose ends and action items

resolved.description: Whether a loose end / question has been resolved
